question.text: Which of the following documents ensures that an individual's assets are distributed according to their wishes after death?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Will is the legal document that specifies how a person's assets should be distributed after death. Without a valid Will, assets are distributed according to applicable succession laws (intestate succession). **The Trap:** Nomination (Option B) only designates who receives the asset immediately — it does not override a Will. POA (Option A) ceases to be valid after death. **Cross-Exam Context:** Estate planning documents are covered in Series X-B (estate planning module) and are introduced in Series XVII for retirement advisers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients to have a registered Will to avoid family disputes. Nominations in bank accounts, MFs, and NPS are for convenience of transfer — the Will ultimately determines rightful ownership.
